stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Coins recovered by archaeologists are divided into two categories.
  • A coin discovered at a documented archaeological excavation is known as a stratified coin.
  • All other coins recovered are known as surface coins.
  • There have been about 8,000 recorded stratified Roman coins in a recent British survey.
  • There have been over 350,000 recorded surface Roman coins in the same survey.
stem: The student wants to contrast the number of stratified Roman coins with the number of surface Roman coins. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: A coin discovered at a documented archaeological excavation is known as a stratified coin; all others are known as surface coins.
Roman coins recovered in Britain are divided into two categories: stratified coins and surface coins.
While the recent British survey recorded only about 8,000 stratified Roman coins, it recorded over 350,000 surface Roman coins.
The recent British survey recorded about 8,000 stratified Roman coins, or coins discovered at documented excavations.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence contrasts the number of stratified Roman coins with the number of surface Roman coins, noting that the recent British survey recorded over 350,000 surface coins but only about 8,000 stratified coins.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence explains the difference between stratified and surface coins, it does not contrast their numbers. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence indicates the two categories of recovered Roman coins; it does not contrast their numbers. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence notes the number of stratified Roman coins recorded, it does not contrast this with the number of surface coins.
